name: Daniel Takabayashi
title: Solutions Engineer / AI Architect
tagline: Event-Driven AI & Multi-Agent Systems for the Enterprise
about:
I build the bridge between AI and production-scale streaming systems. At Confluent, I architect AI-powered streaming pipelines for 50+ enterprise customers and serve as the AI Subject Matter Expert, working on multi-agent systems, MCP integrations, and Flink-based inference architectures that have supported over $15M in deals.

Before that, I co-founded two startups (Boolabs, acquired by B2W Digital in 2016, and Dr. Opinion), led engineering at Overjet where we cut AI inference latency by 20x, and created Marvin AI, the first Brazilian project incubated by the Apache Foundation. It won PwC's Innovation Value Award in 2018.

Published researcher, international conference speaker, and BJJ practitioner. I genuinely believe the best AI systems are event-driven, and I'm still building.

experience.description: Architecting enterprise streaming solutions and leading AI/ML initiatives for major customers across AWS, GCP, and Azure. AI Subject Matter Expert focused on multi-agent systems, MCP integrations, and Flink-based AI architectures.
achievements: Architected end-to-end streaming solutions for 50+ enterprise customers across AWS, GCP, and Azure, reducing ingestion latency and infrastructure costs
Supported deals totaling over $15M+ in the past year as SE and AI SME
Led AI/ML proof-of-concepts including anomaly detection, agentic workflows, MCP Servers, and A2A integrations
Founded and host the Data+AI Monthly Internal Meetup, a recurring series for sharing knowledge on GenAI, multi-agent frameworks, and streaming AI architectures across teams
Created and presented 'Every AI Opportunity is a DSP Opportunity', an internal/external deck on event-driven AI architecture patterns including RAG, MAS, and Flink Agents
Authored technical content on 'CrewAI Crews as Apache Flink Jobs' and 'Flink Multi-Agent System Design', mapping multi-agent concepts to event-driven architectures
Led technical architecture and PoC validation for high-profile LATAM accounts (Blip, iFood, Pipefy, Stix, Michelin, Superlogica) involving RAG, Flink ML inference, and platform modernization
Collaborated with product and engineering to translate customer feedback into roadmap improvements, contributing to Confluent MCP, Flink Agents (FLIP-531), Streaming Agents, and Lightning Tables
Delivered workshops and onboarding for engineering, data science, and DevOps teams on streaming design, partitioning, security (RBAC), and performance tuning
Proactively secured early access to experimental GenAI features (vector search, model inference) for strategic LATAM customers, providing technical validation feedback to engineering

experience.description: Led multiple engineering teams (15+ people) collaborating with directors and senior leadership. Cut AI inference latency by 20x and reduced costs by 6x.
achievements: Managed multiple engineering teams (15+ people) with direct and indirect reports; mentoring, performance reviews, and strategic collaboration with peers at director level and senior leadership
Improved RTA latency of AI inference pipeline by ~20x, enhancing user engagement on the online platform
Drastically reduced unit costs (~6x) by rewriting ML components and optimizing K8s Clusters (GKE) utilization
Redesigned core insurance product component, cutting new customer onboarding from 6+ weeks to hours
Implemented full observability stack (SRE), reducing production incidents by 90% and achieving 99.9% platform uptime
Implemented release process, full CI/CD automation, and observability strategy enabling 4 MLOps engineers to manage hundreds of production services processing millions of images daily
Conceptualized, designed, and built MVP for innovative fraud detection platform (first of its kind) within one quarter, unlocking go-to-market strategies and revenue opportunities

Created Python SDK wrapping ABBYY FineReader Engine (C++ only), enabling ML scientists to prototype OCR solutions at 5x faster speed
Architecture validated and adopted across 10+ legal domain projects before broader enterprise rollout

Designed the platform architecture from scratch to production, serving dental payers across the US

projects.name: Apache Marvin AI Platform
projects.description: First Brazilian open-source project incubated by Apache Foundation. An MLOps platform that helps data science teams ship production ML with a high-scale, low-latency, language-agnostic architecture. Simplifies the path from exploration to deployment.
highlights: Apache Foundation incubator project, the first from Brazil
PwC Innovation Value Award winner (2018), Retail category
Speaker at Apache conferences, QCon, PAPIs, SEI Saturn
~364 combined GitHub stars across Marvin ecosystem, 33 forks, 585 commits
Published paper: 'Marvin — From Exploratory Models to Production' (PAPIs 2017)

projects.name: Scanboo
projects.description: First SaaS platform in Latin America for e-commerce data collection and product classification. A crawler platform for structuring and matching products at scale, handling price monitoring and product deduplication across major e-commerce platforms.

Team of ~20 people, built in 6 months

Price monitoring and product deduplication at massive scale

projects.name: Boomage
projects.description: Deep learning system for visual product recognition using computer vision. A scalable, low-latency API for white-labeled image-based product search across e-commerce platforms, handling millions of classified images with sub-5-second response times.
highlights: First stable version delivered in under 4 months
ML at scale: millions of classified images with 5-second response requirement
Distributed KNN for visual similarity search
GPU-accelerated deep learning pipeline
White-labeled API for e-commerce integration

projects.name: Booska
projects.description: Web product search and price comparison platform. Architected as a web scraping system that independently compares competitor prices across multiple e-commerce sites, structuring large-scale web-derived data.
highlights: Automated price comparison across multiple e-commerce seekers
Web scraping at scale with NLP-based data structuring
Real-time competitor price monitoring
Beta version deployed and validated

projects.name: Julia Architect
projects.description: AI-driven system that assists software architects using case-based reasoning and architectural tactics. Suggests solutions based on non-functional requirements analysis, reusing proven architectural patterns.
highlights: AI-powered architectural decision support
Case-based reasoning for solution reuse
Quality attribute analysis and non-functional requirements mapping
Part of M.S. thesis research at IPT

Cost-effective alternative to Apple/Adobe DRM solutions
Full ecosystem: server, iOS app, Android app, web reader
Custom encryption and DRM implementation

publications.title: Marvin — From Exploratory Models to Production
publications.description: Presents the Marvin AI platform's approach to simplifying ML application development. Covers the path from exploratory data science models to production-ready systems.

publications.title: BooViews: Aspect-based Sentiment Analysis on Product Reviews
publications.venue: PROPOR (International Conference on Computational Processing of Portuguese)
publications.authors: Guilherme Nobre, Daniel Takabayashi et al.
publications.description: Combines SVM and CRF techniques for aspect-based sentiment analysis in Portuguese product reviews. Achieved 91.7% precision in sentiment classification, 74.2% F1-score in aspect extraction, and 79.9% precision in aspect polarity detection.

publications.title: Preprocessing Images to Improve Deep Neural Networks Classification
publications.description: Research on image augmentation and CNN optimization techniques for e-commerce product classification. Explores preprocessing methods that improve deep neural network accuracy for visual recognition tasks.
